---
title: "Some Notes On Malecot 1969 and 'A Pain in the Torus'"
output:
  html_document:
    css: style.css
    theme: cosmo
---

## Notation

Malecot\\Felsenstein | Bob | Meaning
---|-----|-------------------------
$A\subset\mathbb{R}^2$ | $A\subset\mathbb{R}^2$ | Population range
$P,Q\in A$ | $x,y\in A$     | Spatial locations
$\delta(P)$ | $p(x)$  | _Expected_ density of spatial distribution of individuals
$-$      | $P_t$  | _Expected_ msr of spatial distribution of indviduals at time $t$
$dS_P$   | $dx$ | Lebesgue measure at point $P,x$
$f(Q,P)$ | $f(y,x)$ | Dispersal kernel from $y$ to $x$
$g(Q,P)=m(Q-P)$ | $m(x-y)$ | Normalized dispersal kernel from $y$ to $x$

## Obvious results

- $\int_Af(y,x)dx=1\dots$ dispersal is restricted to $A$

- For any mbl $B\subset A$, Bayes rule states

$$\int_Bm(x-y)dx=\frac{\int_Bf(y,x)dP(x)}{\int_Af(y,x)dP(x)}=\frac{\int_Bf(y,x)p(x)dx}{\int_Af(y,x)p(x)dx}$$

- $\int_Am(x-y)dx=1$
